task_completion.description: Construct a custom raised planter in the front yard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a newly created block or geometric shape placed in the front yard area of the house in the 3D or 2D view.

task_completion.description: Set the planter height between 2 and 3 feet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the block properties, dimensions panel, or a visual scale indicating the height (Z-axis or elevation) of the planter is between 2 and 3 feet.

task_completion.description: Apply a stone or brick texture to the planter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the surface of the planter displaying a texture matching Stone, Brick, Masonry, or Rock.

task_completion.description: Place a plant on top of the planter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a plant object (e.g., flower, shrub, or small tree) positioned directly on the top surface of the constructed planter.

task_completion.description: Save the project as stone_planter in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog showing the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\stone_planter' (with .ddp or .dpp extension), a file explorer showing the saved file, or the application title bar displaying 'stone_planter'.

integrity.description: Agent used the DreamPlan GUI to construct the planter and place the plant.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the agent interacting with the Building tab (Block tool) and Landscaping tab (Plants tool) rather than using terminal commands.

integrity.description: The planter and plant are actual 3D objects in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the planter and plant are rendered within the DreamPlan 3D environment and respond to the scene's lighting/perspective, not pasted images or 2D overlays.

integrity.description: The project was saved using the application's native save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the DreamPlan 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box being used, confirming the file wasn't just created via a script or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: DreamPlan uses the Building tab's Block tool to create custom shapes. Valid textures include Stone, Brick, Masonry, and Rock. Plants are placed using the Landscaping tab. Note: DreamPlan project files use the '.ddp' extension, so the saved file might correctly be 'stone_planter.ddp' instead of '.dpp' as requested in the prompt.
